name: The Prisoner's Dilemma
description: Write about two rivals who must choose cooperation or betrayal — without knowing the other's move.

name: Zero‑Sum Thinking
description: Argue for or against the belief that one person's gain is always another's loss.

name: The Element of Surprise
description: Describe how unexpected moves disrupt perfect plans, using a historical or fictional example.

name: Sun Tzu's Terrain
description: Explain how environment (physical, social, digital) determines victory before a single move is made.

name: The Right to Be Forgotten
description: Argue whether people should be able to erase their digital past.

name: Algorithmic Censorship
description: Write about the tension between removing harmful content and protecting free speech.

name: Surveillance Capitalism
description: Critique the business model of selling user data for profit.

name: Digital Afterlife
description: Explore the ethics of AI chatbots that mimic deceased loved ones.

name: Gaslighting as a Tool
description: Write about the deliberate erosion of someone's reality — and how to resist it.

name: The Backfire Effect
description: Describe why people double down on false beliefs when presented with contradictory evidence.

name: Neurolinguistic Framing
description: Explain how word choice (e.g., 'tax relief' vs 'budget adjustment') shapes political opinion.

name: Emotional Contagion
description: Write about how moods spread through groups — from riots to boardroom panics.

name: Panopticon vs Sanctuary
description: Contrast spaces designed for surveillance versus spaces for refuge.

name: Brutalism and Honesty
description: Argue whether raw concrete buildings are oppressive or authentic.

name: The Memory Palace
description: Describe how spatial layouts can enhance memory and learning.

name: Ruin Porn
description: Critique the aesthetic fascination with abandoned industrial sites.

name: Mind Uploading
description: Argue whether a digital copy of your brain would still be you.

name: Cyborg Ethics
description: Write about the line between therapeutic enhancement (prosthetics) and transhumanist upgrade.

name: The Uncanny Valley
description: Explain why near‑human robots and AI provoke disgust instead of empathy.

name: Genetic Class Divide
description: Explore a future where only the rich can afford cognitive or physical enhancement.

name: The Replication Crisis
description: Write about psychology's failure to reproduce landmark studies — and what it means for truth.

name: Conspiracy as Therapy
description: Argue that conspiracy beliefs sometimes fulfill emotional needs better than official narratives.

name: The Death of Expertise
description: Critique the modern distrust of credentialed authorities in favor of amateur opinion.

name: Post‑Truth as Strategy
description: Describe how political actors weaponize ambiguity to make facts irrelevant.

name: The Sublime in War
description: Write about why massive destruction (storms, battles, explosions) can feel beautiful.

name: Gladiatorial Modernity
description: Compare ancient blood sports to modern MMA, esports, or reality TV competition.

name: Ritualized Cruelty
description: Explore how societies legitimize violence through ritual (executions, duels, hazing).

name: The Gaze of Suffering
description: Critique the ethics of photographing or filming victims for art or news.
